stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 30 smartphone models. The sample mean is 241.5 hours with a sample standard deviation of 37.0 hours. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 241.5 hours. 95% CI = [227.7, 255.3]. Point estimate: 241.5 hours.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
